name: VibecodeApp Observable Run Monitor
description:
Implement an event-driven observable run monitor for VibecodeApp.

Product goal:
VibecodeApp should become an observable control layer around OpenCode. The operator should be able to run a task and see two parallel truth surfaces:
1. the OpenCode / agent console output,
2. the VibecodeApp monitor stream showing what VibecodeApp prepared, what prompt/context it gave to the agent, what the agent asked from VibecodeApp via MCP, what changed in the repository, where advisory guards warned, and why.

Important product decision:
Guards are advisory by default. They must not hard-block the run yet. The default behavior should be transparent warning, drift reporting, and review guidance. Strict/blocking mode may be introduced as an explicit option only, but the main default is advisory.

Architectural goal:
Do not bolt a second console onto the existing batch command. Build a small event spine first. CLI, TUI, future GUI, JSONL logs, run replay, and tests must all consume the same run events. UI is only a view over recorded truth.

Core invariants:
- Preserve current CLI behavior unless a task explicitly changes it.
- No hidden enforcement by default.
- Every important run action must be observable as a structured event.
- Every run must be replayable from files under .vibecode/runs/<session_id>/.
- Prompt/context snapshots must be tied to a concrete run, not only stored in .vibecode/current.
- Guard findings must be understandable to a human: what happened, where, why it matters, and what to do next.
- Reviews must be written as artifacts and correction tasks must apply only grounded, actionable fixes.

tasks.title: P0.1 Audit current run, guard, context, MCP, and TUI surfaces
tasks.description:
Create a focused implementation audit before changing behavior.

Inspect the current repository and produce a concise but concrete audit artifact at:
docs/audit/OBSERVABLE_RUN_MONITOR_BASELINE.md

The audit must cover:
- Current CLI entry points related to run/context/guard/check/handoff/dashboard/serve.
- How vibecode run currently creates context packs and OpenCode prompts.
- How vibecode run currently invokes OpenCode or other external platform commands.
- Where stdout/stderr/exit code are captured.
- Where run summary files are written today.
- How guard findings are represented today.
- Whether guard failures currently affect overall status or process exit behavior.
- How MCP server tools are implemented today and whether they can be instrumented.
- How existing TUI/dashboard code is structured and whether it can be reused.
- Current tests that cover run/guard/context/MCP/TUI behavior.
- Known risk areas and likely files to touch.

Do not implement the monitor in this task. This is a baseline truth record only.
Run at least:
- python -m compileall vibecode
- pytest, or the narrowest available test command if full pytest is too slow.
Record exactly what was run and whether it passed.

tasks.title: P0.2 Review baseline audit
tasks.description:
Review docs/audit/OBSERVABLE_RUN_MONITOR_BASELINE.md and the current code.

Write review output to:
docs/audit/OBSERVABLE_RUN_MONITOR_BASELINE_REVIEW.md

Check:
- The audit is grounded in actual code, not guessed architecture.
- The audit identifies the real run path and not only README claims.
- The audit accurately describes current guard advisory/blocking behavior.
- The audit identifies stale or contradictory docs if present.
- The audit gives a realistic list of files to change.
- The audit does not secretly implement product changes.

Do not modify source code except the review document. If the audit is materially wrong, mark findings as BLOCKER with exact files/functions to inspect.

tasks.title: P0.3 Fix baseline audit issues if review found any
tasks.description:
Read docs/audit/OBSERVABLE_RUN_MONITOR_BASELINE_REVIEW.md.

If the review contains actionable issues, update docs/audit/OBSERVABLE_RUN_MONITOR_BASELINE.md so it becomes accurate.
If the review found no issues, write a short no-op note to:
docs/audit/OBSERVABLE_RUN_MONITOR_BASELINE_FIX.md

Do not change runtime behavior in this task.
Run compile/tests only if source files were touched.

tasks.title: P1.1 Implement structured event spine
tasks.description:
Implement the foundational event model for observable runs.

Likely new file:
vibecode/events.py

Required concepts:
- VibecodeEvent as a serializable dataclass or equivalent typed structure.
- Stable event fields:
  - event_id
  - session_id
  - timestamp
  - type
  - level: debug/info/warning/error
  - message
  - data
- Event type constants or enum-like names for run lifecycle, context, prompt, agent process, MCP, git delta, guard, checks, handoff, and summary.
- EventSink protocol/interface with emit(event).
- InMemoryEventSink for tests.
- JsonlEventSink that appends one JSON object per line.
- ConsoleEventSink that can print compact readable messages.
- MultiEventSink that fans out events to multiple sinks.
- NullEventSink for call sites that do not care.
- Helper function to create events consistently, including timestamp/session_id.

Design rules:
- Keep this module dependency-light and boring.
- Do not import high-level run/guard/context modules from events.py.
- JSON serialization must be deterministic enough for tests.
- Event data must accept plain JSON-compatible values only.
- If non-JSON values are passed, either convert safely or fail with a clear error.

Tests:
Add focused tests for:
- event serialization roundtrip,
- JSONL append behavior,
- multi sink fan-out,
- in-memory sink capture,
- invalid/non-serializable data handling if implemented.

Run:
- python -m compileall vibecode
- pytest tests related to events, or full pytest if practical.

tasks.title: P1.2 Review structured event spine
tasks.description:
Review the event spine implementation.

Write review output to:
docs/audit/OBSERVABLE_RUN_MONITOR_P1_EVENT_SPINE_REVIEW.md

Check:
- events.py is not coupled to high-level runtime modules.
- Event schema includes enough fields for replay and future GUI/TUI rendering.
- JSONL writing is append-safe and creates parent directories when needed.
- Tests actually prove serialization and sink behavior.
- No direct product behavior changed accidentally.
- No broad refactors were introduced.

Do not modify implementation files. Only write the review document.

tasks.title: P1.3 Apply event spine review fixes
tasks.description:
Read docs/audit/OBSERVABLE_RUN_MONITOR_P1_EVENT_SPINE_REVIEW.md.

Apply only actionable fixes from the review. Do not expand scope.
If no fixes are needed, write:
docs/audit/OBSERVABLE_RUN_MONITOR_P1_EVENT_SPINE_FIX.md
with a no-op explanation.

Run:
- python -m compileall vibecode
- relevant event tests
- any affected existing tests.

tasks.title: P2.1 Implement per-run session paths and durable run artifacts
tasks.description:
Implement a small run session artifact layer.

Goal:
Every observable run must have a stable folder under:
.vibecode/runs/<session_id>/

Likely new or changed module:
vibecode/session_log.py
or a similarly named low-level module.

Required behavior:
- Create or resolve a run session directory.
- Define paths for:
  - events.jsonl
  - summary.json
  - opencode_prompt.md
  - context_pack.md
  - guard_report.json
  - guard_report.md
  - checks_report.json
  - handoff_report.json or handoff_report.md if applicable
  - agent_stdout.log
  - agent_stderr.log
- Provide safe helpers to copy/snapshot current prompt and context files into the run directory.
- Do not remove existing .vibecode/current behavior yet; run snapshots are additive.
- Provide a way for run code to create JsonlEventSink pointing to the session events.jsonl.
- Ensure paths work on Windows.
- Ensure parent directories are created.
- Do not introduce global mutable session state unless absolutely necessary.

Tests:
- session directory creation,
- expected paths,
- snapshot copy behavior,
- Windows-safe path assumptions where possible,
- no crash when optional source file is absent if that behavior is chosen.

Run compile and relevant tests.

tasks.title: P2.2 Review session artifact layer
tasks.description:
Review the session artifact implementation.

Write review output to:
docs/audit/OBSERVABLE_RUN_MONITOR_P2_SESSION_ARTIFACTS_REVIEW.md

Check:
- Paths are under .vibecode/runs/<session_id>, not scattered.
- Existing .vibecode/current behavior is preserved.
- Snapshot behavior is explicit and tested.
- No brittle absolute paths.
- No accidental deletion of current/generated/runtime artifacts.
- Tests cover missing parent directories and normal snapshot flow.

Do not modify implementation files. Only write the review document.

tasks.title: P2.3 Apply session artifact review fixes
tasks.description:
Read docs/audit/OBSERVABLE_RUN_MONITOR_P2_SESSION_ARTIFACTS_REVIEW.md.

Apply only grounded fixes. If no fixes are needed, write:
docs/audit/OBSERVABLE_RUN_MONITOR_P2_SESSION_ARTIFACTS_FIX.md

Run compile and affected tests.

tasks.title: P3.1 Refactor run command around observable run controller
tasks.description:
Refactor vibecode run so it can emit structured events without breaking normal CLI use.

Goal:
Create a RunController or equivalent orchestration class/function that receives:
- repo root/config,
- task,
- platform,
- optional model/profile/permissions args if currently supported,
- event sink,
- session artifact paths.

It should orchestrate the same high-level behavior as the current run command:
- git/preflight status,
- index freshness decision,
- context pack generation,
- prompt generation,
- external agent invocation,
- post-run guard/check/handoff,
- summary writing.

Required event emissions:
- RunStarted
- GitPreflightStarted / GitPreflightCompleted / GitPreflightWarning
- IndexCheckStarted / IndexCheckCompleted
- ContextPackStarted / ContextPackWritten
- PromptWritten
- AgentStarted
- AgentFinished
- GuardStarted / GuardCompleted
- ChecksStarted / ChecksCompleted
- HandoffStarted / HandoffCompleted
- RunSummaryWritten
- RunFinished

Rules:
- Avoid a giant rewrite. Extract, then instrument.
- Existing command-line behavior should remain usable.
- If event sink is absent, use NullEventSink.
- The CLI may still print a compact summary, but important truth must come from events and run artifacts.
- Do not implement live TUI yet.
- Do not change guard advisory/blocking semantics yet unless strictly necessary; that is P5.

Tests:
- use fake/minimal platform invocation where possible,
- prove a run emits a sensible ordered set of events,
- prove summary is still written,
- prove failures produce error events,
- prove legacy CLI path still works at least through narrow tests.

Run compile and affected tests.

tasks.title: P3.2 Review run controller refactor
tasks.description:
Review the run controller refactor.

Write review output to:
docs/audit/OBSERVABLE_RUN_MONITOR_P3_RUN_CONTROLLER_REVIEW.md

Check:
- Behavior preservation: no accidental change to existing run semantics.
- Event emissions are meaningful and not low-value spam.
- The refactor did not create a god object worse than the original.
- Failure paths emit useful events.
- Tests use fake subprocess/platform paths and do not require real OpenCode.
- Session artifacts and events are connected properly.
- There is no hidden hard enforcement added.

Do not modify implementation files. Only write the review document.

tasks.title: P3.3 Apply run controller review fixes
tasks.description:
Read docs/audit/OBSERVABLE_RUN_MONITOR_P3_RUN_CONTROLLER_REVIEW.md.

Apply only actionable fixes from the review.
If no fixes are needed, write:
docs/audit/OBSERVABLE_RUN_MONITOR_P3_RUN_CONTROLLER_FIX.md

tasks.title: P4.1 Add streaming agent process output
tasks.description:
Change external agent execution from purely batch-style capture to streaming-capable execution.

Goal:
When OpenCode runs, VibecodeApp should be able to stream agent stdout/stderr events live while still preserving final logs and exit code.

Required behavior:
- Add a streaming process runner abstraction if none exists.
- Use subprocess.Popen or equivalent to read stdout/stderr incrementally.
- Emit AgentStdout events for stdout lines/chunks.
- Emit AgentStderr events for stderr lines/chunks.
- Write full stdout to .vibecode/runs/<session_id>/agent_stdout.log.
- Write full stderr to .vibecode/runs/<session_id>/agent_stderr.log.
- Preserve final exit code.
- Preserve existing batch behavior for non-monitor CLI if needed, but prefer one implementation path.
- Handle Windows safely.
- Avoid deadlock when both stdout and stderr produce output.
- Do not attempt full interactive PTY/ConPTY support in this task. This is streaming output MVP, not an embedded terminal.

Tests:
- fake process that prints stdout and exits 0,
- fake process that prints stderr and exits nonzero,
- mixed stdout/stderr if practical,
- verify events and log files.

Run compile and affected tests.

tasks.title: P4.2 Review streaming agent output
tasks.description:
Review the streaming process implementation.

Write review output to:
docs/audit/OBSERVABLE_RUN_MONITOR_P4_STREAMING_AGENT_REVIEW.md

Check:
- No stdout/stderr deadlock risk.
- Windows compatibility was considered.
- Exit code truth is preserved.
- Output is both emitted as events and saved as logs.
- Tests do not depend on real OpenCode.
- The implementation does not pretend to support full interactive terminal if it only supports stream capture.

Do not modify implementation files. Only write the review document.

tasks.title: P4.3 Apply streaming agent review fixes
tasks.description:
Read docs/audit/OBSERVABLE_RUN_MONITOR_P4_STREAMING_AGENT_REVIEW.md.

Apply only actionable fixes.
If no fixes are needed, write:
docs/audit/OBSERVABLE_RUN_MONITOR_P4_STREAMING_AGENT_FIX.md

Run compile and affected tests.

tasks.title: P5.1 Make guard behavior advisory by default
tasks.description:
Update run-level guard handling so guard findings are warning/reporting truth by default, not hard enforcement.

Product rule:
Guard must say what failed, where drift happened, and why it matters. But default run behavior must not hard-block solely because guard found issues.

Required behavior:
- Introduce an explicit guard mode concept if useful:
  - advisory/default
  - strict/blocking optional
- In advisory mode:
  - guard findings are preserved,
  - severity is preserved,
  - run summary says something like needs_review or completed_with_warnings when appropriate,
  - OpenCode exit code truth is not hidden,
  - guard warnings do not automatically convert the whole run into failure.
- In strict mode, if implemented:
  - preserve existing hard-fail behavior or a clearly documented blocking behavior.
- CLI help/docs must make clear that advisory is default.
- Existing tests that expect guard failure to equal run failure must be updated only if they represent old default semantics.
- Add tests proving advisory guard does not hard-block.

Be careful:
Do not water down the findings themselves. Severity can still be error. The difference is default enforcement policy, not truth.

Run compile and affected tests.

tasks.title: P5.2 Review advisory guard semantics
tasks.description:
Review the advisory guard change.

Write review output to:
docs/audit/OBSERVABLE_RUN_MONITOR_P5_ADVISORY_GUARD_REVIEW.md

Check:
- Advisory mode does not hide serious guard findings.
- Run status language is honest and not misleading.
- Agent exit code remains visible.
- Strict behavior, if added, is explicit and documented.
- Tests prove the new default.
- No unrelated guard rules were weakened.
- No accidental bypass of checks/handoff reporting.

Do not modify implementation files. Only write the review document.

tasks.title: P5.3 Apply advisory guard review fixes
tasks.description:
Read docs/audit/OBSERVABLE_RUN_MONITOR_P5_ADVISORY_GUARD_REVIEW.md.

Apply only actionable fixes.
If no fixes are needed, write:
docs/audit/OBSERVABLE_RUN_MONITOR_P5_ADVISORY_GUARD_FIX.md

Run compile and affected tests.

tasks.title: P6.1 Improve guard findings into human-readable drift reports
tasks.description:
Improve guard/report output so the operator can understand exactly what happened.

Goal:
Guard reporting must be useful in the Vibecode monitor panel. It cannot be a vague fail/pass blob.

For each finding, expose or derive:
- severity,
- rule_id,
- category/domain,
- file path or affected area,
- concise title,
- what happened,
- why it matters,
- suggested fix,
- evidence/context,
- related check/test if known.

Required outputs:
- machine-readable guard_report.json in the run session folder,
- human-readable guard_report.md in the run session folder,
- GuardFindingEmitted events for individual findings or grouped findings,
- GuardCompleted event with counts by severity/category.

UI/report style:
- Group findings by severity and category.
- Keep messages concise but actionable.
- Include exact paths.
- Do not bury the operator in raw JSON unless they ask for detail.

Tests:
- report generated when findings exist,
- report generated or skipped clearly when no findings exist,
- grouped counts are correct,
- event data includes key fields.

Run compile and affected tests.

tasks.title: P6.2 Review human-readable guard reporting
tasks.description:
Review the guard report improvements.

Write review output to:
docs/audit/OBSERVABLE_RUN_MONITOR_P6_GUARD_REPORT_REVIEW.md

Check:
- Findings are understandable to a human.
- JSON and Markdown reports agree.
- The report does not lose severity/rule identity.
- No huge raw blobs are dumped into the monitor path.
- Tests cover both findings and no-findings cases.
- Report generation works even when guard partially fails.

Do not modify implementation files. Only write the review document.

tasks.title: P6.3 Apply guard report review fixes
tasks.description:
Read docs/audit/OBSERVABLE_RUN_MONITOR_P6_GUARD_REPORT_REVIEW.md.

Apply only actionable fixes.
If no fixes are needed, write:
docs/audit/OBSERVABLE_RUN_MONITOR_P6_GUARD_REPORT_FIX.md

tasks.title: P7.1 Add prompt and context snapshot events
tasks.description:
Make prompt/context truth first-class in observable runs.

Required behavior:
- When a context pack is generated, emit event with:
  - path to current context pack,
  - path to run snapshot,
  - approximate size,
  - task summary,
  - included sections if available.
- When an OpenCode prompt is generated, emit event with:
  - path to current prompt,
  - path to run snapshot,
  - approximate size,
  - platform/model/profile if available.
- Copy prompt and context pack into .vibecode/runs/<session_id>/.
- The monitor must be able to show where the exact prompt is stored.
- Do not always dump full prompt into the live event message. Store it in artifact file and put path/summary into event data.
- Add tests ensuring prompt/context snapshots are tied to the session.

Run compile and affected tests.

tasks.title: P7.2 Review prompt/context snapshot truth
tasks.description:
Review prompt/context snapshot behavior.

Write review output to:
docs/audit/OBSERVABLE_RUN_MONITOR_P7_PROMPT_CONTEXT_REVIEW.md

Check:
- The exact prompt/context used by the run is preserved.
- Events point to snapshot files, not only .vibecode/current.
- No giant prompt content is spammed into every event.
- Existing context generation behavior is preserved.
- Tests prove per-run snapshot behavior.

Do not modify implementation files. Only write the review document.

tasks.title: P7.3 Apply prompt/context review fixes
tasks.description:

tasks.title: P8.1 Add MCP tool-call observability
tasks.description:
Instrument VibecodeApp MCP tools so the operator can see what the agent asked VibecodeApp.

Goal:
When OpenCode or another agent calls VibecodeApp MCP tools, VibecodeApp should log:
- tool name,
- sanitized arguments,
- result summary,
- error if any,
- timestamp,
- optional session_id if available.

Required behavior:
- Add event/logging hooks around MCP tools such as get_file_card, find_symbol, list_high_risk if they exist.
- Emit or write:
  - McpToolCalled
  - McpToolReturned
  - McpToolFailed
- If MCP server runs out-of-process from the run controller, use a safe fallback log path such as .vibecode/logs/mcp_events.jsonl.
- If a VIBECODE_SESSION_ID environment variable or similar session correlation can be passed to the MCP server, implement it narrowly and document it.
- Do not dump huge MCP responses. Store a compact result summary:
  - number of symbols,
  - number of files,
  - risk count,
  - approximate chars,
  - path queried,
  - error message if failed.
- Preserve full tool correctness.

Tests:
- direct tool call produces log/event,
- failures are logged,
- result summary avoids large blobs,
- no MCP dependency import failure is introduced in normal non-MCP flows.

Run compile and affected tests.

tasks.title: P8.2 Review MCP observability
tasks.description:
Review MCP observability.

Write review output to:
docs/audit/OBSERVABLE_RUN_MONITOR_P8_MCP_OBSERVABILITY_REVIEW.md

Check:
- Tool calls and returns are visible.
- Sensitive or huge result payloads are not dumped into the monitor.
- MCP session correlation is honest; if correlation is best-effort, document it.
- Non-MCP CLI usage still works if MCP optional dependencies are absent.
- Tests cover success and failure logging.

Do not modify implementation files. Only write the review document.

tasks.title: P8.3 Apply MCP observability review fixes
tasks.description:

tasks.title: P9.1 Implement TUI two-pane monitor MVP
tasks.description:
Implement the first operator-facing monitor command.

Goal:
A TUI command should run an OpenCode task and show two live panes:
- left pane: OpenCode/agent stdout and stderr stream,
- right pane: VibecodeApp event stream and advisory warnings.

Preferred command shape:
vibecode monitor --platform opencode --task "..."

If existing CLI style suggests a different command shape, keep it consistent and document it.

MVP layout:
- Header with repo root, session id, task, platform/model.
- Left text pane for AgentStdout/AgentStderr.
- Right text pane for Vibecode events.
- Bottom/status area showing:
  - agent status,
  - guard status,
  - checks status,
  - run artifact path.
- Key binding or simple instruction to open/view prompt path is optional, not required.
- The TUI can be basic. Do not overbuild.
- It may reuse Textual if already present.
- It must consume events from the same event spine, not parse random printed text.
- If full interactive OpenCode terminal is not supported, document that this MVP streams output and is not a PTY.

Fallback:
If Textual integration is too risky, implement a minimal non-interactive live monitor mode first, but prefer proper TUI if the project already has Textual.

Tests:
- unit tests for event-to-panel formatting if practical,
- command registration/import test,
- non-UI smoke path with fake runner if possible.

Run compile and affected tests.

tasks.title: P9.2 Review TUI monitor MVP
tasks.description:
Review the TUI monitor implementation.

Write review output to:
docs/audit/OBSERVABLE_RUN_MONITOR_P9_TUI_MONITOR_REVIEW.md

Check:
- It uses the event spine rather than scraping CLI output.
- It clearly separates agent output from VibecodeApp events.
- It does not claim full interactive terminal support if not implemented.
- It shows guard warnings clearly.
- It shows prompt/context artifact paths.
- It does not break existing dashboard or run commands.
- Tests cover import/formatting enough to prevent immediate rot.

Do not modify implementation files. Only write the review document.

tasks.description:

docs/audit/OBSERVABLE_RUN_MONITOR_P9_TUI_MONITOR_FIX.md

tasks.title: P10.1 Add run replay/show command
tasks.description:
Add a small command to inspect a previous observable run from its artifacts.

Goal:
Because events are saved to events.jsonl, the operator should be able to review a run after it finishes.

Preferred command examples:
- vibecode show-run <session_id>
- vibecode runs list
or use the existing CLI conventions if different.

Required behavior:
- List recent run session ids if implementing list.
- Show summary for a selected run:
  - task,
  - platform/model,
  - start/end,
  - agent exit code,
  - guard counts,
  - checks status,
  - artifact paths.
- Optionally replay event messages in chronological order.
- Do not require rerunning the agent.
- Handle missing/corrupt events.jsonl gracefully.

Tests:
- parse events.jsonl,
- show summary from artifacts,
- missing run id error is clear.

Run compile and affected tests.

tasks.title: P10.2 Review run replay/show command
tasks.description:

Write review output to:
docs/audit/OBSERVABLE_RUN_MONITOR_P10_RUN_REPLAY_REVIEW.md

Check:
- It reads artifacts instead of re-executing anything.
- Missing/corrupt run data is handled honestly.
- It shows enough information to debug what happened.
- It does not expose raw huge payloads by default.
- Tests cover normal and missing/corrupt cases.

Do not modify implementation files. Only write the review document.

tasks.title: P11.1 Update documentation and AGENTS guidance
tasks.description:
Update user-facing and agent-facing docs to match the implemented observable monitor reality.

Likely files:
- README.md
- docs/QUICKSTART.md
- AGENTS.md
- possibly docs/CONTROL_LAYER_FINAL_AUDIT.md or a new docs/audit status document

Required docs content:
- Explain VibecodeApp observable run monitor concept.
- Explain default advisory guard behavior.
- Explain strict mode only if implemented.
- Explain the two-pane TUI monitor command.
- Explain where run artifacts are written.
- Explain where prompt/context snapshots are stored.
- Explain how to inspect a previous run.
- Explain MCP observability if implemented and any limitations.
- Clearly say whether the monitor is streaming stdout/stderr only or a true interactive PTY.
- Remove or flag stale claims contradicted by implementation.

Do not oversell. If something is not implemented, say it is not implemented.
Run docs-related checks if the repo has any.

tasks.description:

docs/audit/OBSERVABLE_RUN_MONITOR_P11_DOCS_REVIEW.md

- Run artifact paths are accurate.

tasks.title: P12.1 Final validation and dogfood report
tasks.description:
Run final validation and write an end-to-end dogfood report.

Required validation:
- python -m compileall vibecode
- full pytest if practical; otherwise explain why not and run the broadest safe subset
- a fake/safe monitor run if possible without requiring paid/real OpenCode
- a real OpenCode smoke run only if available and safe in the environment
- show-run/replay command smoke if implemented
- verify run folder contains:
  - events.jsonl
  - summary.json
  - prompt/context snapshots when generated
  - agent logs when an agent run happened
  - guard report when guard ran

Write:
docs/audit/OBSERVABLE_RUN_MONITOR_FINAL_DOGFOOD_REPORT.md

Report must include:
- exact commands run,
- pass/fail results,
- known limitations,
- any skipped checks and why,
- whether the product goal is met,
- next recommended work.

Do not hide failures. If something is broken, say exactly what.

tasks.title: P12.2 Final independent review
tasks.description:
Perform final independent review of the observable monitor implementation and dogfood report.

Write:
docs/audit/OBSERVABLE_RUN_MONITOR_FINAL_REVIEW.md

Check:
- Product goal is actually met or honestly reported as partial.
- Event spine is used across run/TUI/MCP paths where claimed.
- Guard is advisory by default.
- Run artifacts are durable and replayable.
- Prompt/context truth is preserved per run.
- Two-pane monitor works at MVP level or limitations are explicit.
- Tests are credible.
- There are no obvious architecture violations or hidden brittle shortcuts.

Do not modify implementation files. Only write the final review.

tasks.title: P12.3 Apply final review fixes and close implementation
tasks.description:
Read docs/audit/OBSERVABLE_RUN_MONITOR_FINAL_REVIEW.md.

Apply only final review fixes that are concrete and safe.
If the review identifies large follow-up work, do not start a new large refactor; document it under:
docs/audit/OBSERVABLE_RUN_MONITOR_FOLLOWUPS.md

Run final validation again after any source changes:
- python -m compileall vibecode
- affected tests
- full pytest if practical

If no concrete fixes are needed, write a no-op note to:
docs/audit/OBSERVABLE_RUN_MONITOR_FINAL_FIX.md

Update docs/audit/OBSERVABLE_RUN_MONITOR_FINAL_DOGFOOD_REPORT.md with final post-fix status if needed.
